body.title: [escalated] Sweep stale lab/claudecode refs -> devlab/claudecode (scoped, not blind sed)
body.description:
**Problem:** lab/claudecode was renamed devlab/claudecode, but ~46 stale references remain across the repo (30 are docstrings/comments; the rest are functional paths, test data, or hook scripts). Two collection-breaking test refs were already fixed (commit on 2026-06-19); this ticket sweeps the remainder. Found incidentally during the build-queue cutover.

**CRITICAL — NOT a blind sed of /lab/.** lab/ was only PARTIALLY renamed. These dirs are STILL LIVE and must never be touched:
  - lab/design_docs/  (read by /sorted, decisions live here)
  - lab/design_docs_for_igor/  (decisions_log.dsb — written by /sorted and /day-close)
Only the claudecode subpath moved. Target ONLY: `lab/claudecode` -> `devlab/claudecode` and the dotted module form `lab.claudecode` -> `devlab.claudecode`.

**EXCLUDE (leave as-is — these are intentional, not stale):**
  - tests/test_agentctl_init.py — builds FAKE lab/claudecode trees as test fixtures to exercise path-detection logic; the string is test DATA, not a path to fix.
  - tests/test_repo_auditor.py — uses lab/claudecode/... inside Affected-files parse-input strings; arbitrary test data.
  - Historical/archival COMMENTS (e.g. "kept as historical artifact", "on this machine lab/claudecode is in TheIgors") — describe the past, correct as written.
  - Anything under .venv/ or __pycache__/.
When unsure whether a ref is functional vs intentional: LEAVE IT and list it in the result for human review. Do not guess.

**Method (cheaper than an interactive search — that is the point):**
  1. Enumerate: `grep -rn "lab/claudecode\|lab\.claudecode" --include=*.py --include=*.sh --include=*.md --include=*.yaml . | grep -vi devlab | grep -v "/.venv/\|/__pycache__/"`
  2. Classify each hit: functional-code-path | docstring/comment | intentional-fixture (exclude) | historical-comment (exclude).
  3. Replace functional + cosmetic-docstring hits `lab/claudecode`->`devlab/claudecode` and `lab.claudecode`->`devlab.claudecode`. sed is fine PER-FILE on the classified set; do NOT sed the whole tree blindly.
  4. Known non-.py hits to review: CLAUDE.md, tests/hooks/test_pre_commit_guard.sh, tests/igor/hooks/test_pre_commit_guard.sh, devlab/claudecode/cc_skills/query-ticket/SKILL.md, devlab/claudecode/hooks/session-start/igor-env-check.sh.
  5. SPECIAL CASE — verify functional impact: tests/igor/test_igor_admin.py asserts decision_manager.py source references "lab"+"claudecode". If decision_manager.py was migrated to devlab, that assertion now tests the wrong thing — check decision_manager.py and fix BOTH the source path and the assertion together (or flag if ambiguous).

**Affected files:** ~25-30 across tests/, devlab/claudecode/, CLAUDE.md, hook scripts. Exact set comes from step 1.
**Design rules:** Surgical, scoped replacement only. lab/design_docs and lab/design_docs_for_igor are off-limits. Skip intentional fixtures and historical comments.
**Scope boundary:** IN — stale lab/claudecode|lab.claudecode references. OUT — any other lab/ subpath; the design-doc dirs; renaming actual files.
**Completion criteria:** `grep -rn "lab/claudecode\|lab\.claudecode" --include=*.py . | grep -vi devlab | grep -v "/.venv/\|fixture-allowlist"` returns only the documented intentional/historical exclusions (list them in the result). Full pytest collection of touched test files passes.
**Test plan:** Run pytest on every touched test file (at minimum: any test whose sys.path or imports changed). Confirm 0 collection errors. Confirm test_igor_admin.py passes after the decision_manager.py check.

## Escalation summary
**What was tried:** {"status": "error", "result": "MAX_TURNS: hit 80 turns without terminal response", "error_class": "MAX_TURNS", "error_number": 80}
**Where it broke:** result missing DONE: prefix — not a completion
**What now?**
